title: aion-store crate, EventStore trait, and StoreError
purpose: Define the single persistence contract for Aion. The EventStore trait is what the engine talks to and what every backend implements, including external ones.
task: Create the aion-store leaf crate depending only on aion-core. Define StoreError as a closed enum. Define the EventStore async trait with append (expected-sequence guard), read_history, list_active, query, schedule_timer, and expired_timers. Define the TimerEntry type the timer methods exchange.

requirements.title: Crate scaffold and StoreError
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL create the aion-store crate depending only on aion-core (no other Aion crate). THE SYSTEM SHALL define StoreError as a closed enum with SequenceConflict, NotFound, Backend, and Serialization variants.
acceptance: crates/aion-store exists, is a workspace member, and depends on aion-core plus external crates only (async-trait or native async, thiserror, serde).
src/lib.rs contains only declarations and re-exports.
StoreError has variants SequenceConflict { expected, found }, NotFound { workflow_id }, Backend(String or boxed error), Serialization(String).
StoreError derives Debug, implements std::error::Error and Display, and is Send + Sync.

requirements.title: EventStore trait — event methods
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define EventStore as an async trait that is Send + Sync + 'static, with append (taking an expected sequence and applying events atomically), read_history, list_active, and query.
acceptance: store.rs defines the EventStore trait, async, bound Send + Sync + 'static.

append(&self, workflow_id: &WorkflowId, events: &[Event], expected_seq: u64) -> Result<(), StoreError>, documented as atomic (all events apply or none) and returning SequenceConflict when the stored head does not equal expected_seq.
read_history(&self, workflow_id: &WorkflowId) -> Result<Vec<Event>, StoreError> returns events in sequence order.
list_active(&self) -> Result<Vec<WorkflowId>, StoreError> returns non-terminal workflows.
query(&self, filter: &WorkflowFilter) -> Result<Vec<WorkflowSummary>, StoreError> returns matching summaries.
Trait method docs state the contract precisely, including the atomicity and sequence-guard semantics.

requirements.title: Durable timer methods and TimerEntry
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define TimerEntry and add schedule_timer and expired_timers to EventStore. schedule_timer SHALL persist a durable timer; expired_timers SHALL return timers due to fire as of a given instant.
acceptance: timer.rs defines TimerEntry { workflow_id: WorkflowId, timer_id: TimerId, fire_at: DateTime<Utc> } deriving the standard traits.
EventStore gains schedule_timer(&self, &WorkflowId, &TimerId, fire_at: DateTime<Utc>) -> Result<(), StoreError>.
EventStore gains expired_timers(&self, as_of: DateTime<Utc>) -> Result<Vec<TimerEntry>, StoreError> returning timers whose fire_at <= as_of.
Docs state that scheduling a timer is part of the same logical transaction as the TimerStarted event the engine appends.
The trait remains object-safe (usable as Arc<dyn EventStore>).

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the trait — InMemoryStore lands in AC-006.
SHALL NOT depend on beamr, the engine, or any storage library (libSQL, Postgres).
SHALL NOT split timers into a separate trait; they live on EventStore per design decision D6.
verification: EventStore is object-safe — a compile test constructs an Arc<dyn EventStore> (against a trivial stub or deferred to AC-006).
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
